seoTitle: How to Edit a Zendesk Theme (No-Code and Code)
metaDescription: Customize your Zendesk Help Center theme with no-code branding tools or dive into the code editor for deeper CSS and template changes, safely staged.
intro: A Help Center that clashes with your brand can hurt your credibility with customers. This guide covers safely duplicating your live theme into a draft, making quick no-code branding changes, and diving into the code editor for deeper structural edits before publishing.

steps.heading: Duplicate your live theme
steps.body: Create a draft copy of your current production theme so you can experiment without risking an outage.

steps.heading: Use the no-code settings panel
steps.body: Launch the theme customizer to quickly adjust brand colors, fonts, logos, and hero banner images.

steps.heading: Open the code editor for deeper changes
steps.body: For structural edits, click Edit Code to access the local template directory, including HTML markup and stylesheets.

steps.heading: Edit CSS and JavaScript files
steps.body: Adjust the theme's style.css and script.js files to inject custom styling or scripted behavior.

steps.heading: Preview across devices
steps.body: Use the live multi-role preview channels to check how your theme displays on both desktop and mobile viewports.

steps.heading: Publish your changes
steps.body: Once you're satisfied with the preview, click Set as Live Theme to push your edits to your public Help Center.

faq.question: Will editing my theme affect my published articles?
faq.answer: No, theme edits change layout and styling, not the underlying article content, especially when you work from a draft copy first.

faq.question: Do I need coding knowledge to rebrand my Help Center?
faq.answer: Not necessarily — the no-code settings panel covers common changes like colors, fonts, and logos without touching any code.

faq.question: What's the safest way to make theme changes?
faq.answer: Duplicating your live theme into a draft before editing lets you experiment freely without risking your published Help Center.

faq.question: Can I preview my theme on mobile before publishing?
faq.answer: Yes, the live multi-role preview tools let you check how your theme renders across desktop and mobile viewports before going live.
